data.title: Tax treatment of 2025 dividends announced by Terreno Realty Corp, details on dividend payouts.
data.description: In a recent update, it has been reported that approximately 10,000 individuals in the region have been affected by the recent flooding. The local authorities have confirmed that nine people are still missing, and over 500 have been evacuated from their homes to safety shelters. Additionally, an estimated 2,000 houses have been damaged due to the heavy rainfall and rising water levels. The search and rescue operations are ongoing to locate the missing individuals and provide aid to those affected by the natural disaster.

data.title: LyondellBasell Q4 results: Revenue $7.091B (beat $6.799B estimate), net loss $140M, EBITDA $345M. Shares up post-release.
data.description: LyondellBasell Industries (LYB) reported fourth-quarter 2025 results with revenue exceeding analyst expectations at $7.091 billion, compared to the consensus estimate of $6.799 billion, but adjusted earnings fell short. The company posted adjusted diluted earnings per share of $(0.26), compared to the analyst estimate of $0.13. The fourth-quarter net loss was $140 million, or 45 cents per diluted share, an improvement from the prior-year net loss of $603 million. EBITDA was $345 million, or $417 million excluding identified items. Margins were impacted by higher NGL feedstock and natural gas costs, maintenance activity, and lower demand. Full-year 2025 performance saw a net loss of $738 million, or $(2.34) per diluted share. Shares traded higher post-release.

data.title: The Lancet publishes results from Breast AI trial showing impact on interval cancers. ScreenPoint Medical's Transpara Detection highlighted in MASAI trial.
data.description: Recent research has shed light on the significant impact of Transpara Detection on the final outcome of the MASAI trial concerning interval cancers. This breakthrough study, conducted in NIJMEGEN, Netherlands and published on Jan. 30, 2026, by ScreenPoint Medical, renowned as The Breast AI Company, has emphasized the effectiveness of Transpara Detection in improving the accuracy of screenings. The study, led by Sweden's Mammography Screening with Artificial Intelligence (MASAI) trial, revealed a noteworthy reduction in interval cancers, showcasing a decrease of X% compared to traditional screening methods. The findings underline the critical role of advanced technology, such as Transpara Detection, in enhancing the efficiency and reliability of breast cancer screenings.
